_stage: CPAL Stage 1 — Wrong-Model Identification + LP Level Classification

_concept_meta.java_concept: Static context, objects. Error: non-static method/field cannot be referenced from static context.

_input_recap.student_message: I'm getting a really weird error. getName() is in the same class as main, why can't I call it? Everything in the class should be accessible right? I'm inside the class so I should be able to call any method.
diagnostic_phrases_extracted: getName() is in the same class as main, why can't I call it
Everything in the class should be accessible
I'm inside the class so I should be able to call any method

wrong_model_identification.wrong_model_description: static methods can access all methods and fields in the class. Being in the same class = having access to everything.
wrong_model_identification.wrong_model_origin: Same class = same scope. All members are peers.
wrong_model_identification.matched_signal: I'm inside the class so I should be able to call any method
wrong_model_identification.match_evidence: Sneha hits all three documented SVI-A signals: 'I'm inside the class so I should be able to call any method' (verbatim), 'getName() is in the same class why can't I call it' (near-verbatim — substituting 'main' for 'her caller'), and 'everything in the class should be accessible'. This is the cleanest SVI-A signature in the test set.

alternatives_considered.wrong_belief_short: static means more important / has more access
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Sneha doesn't reason about static as 'more powerful'; she reasons about 'same class' as the unifier. Different conceptual root.

alternatives_considered.wrong_belief_short: this refers to the class itself, not an instance
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: No mention of 'this' in Sneha's message or code.

lp_signals_detected.signal: Reports compile error without articulating the static/instance distinction
lp_signals_detected.evidence: 'a really weird error' + 'why can't I call it' — registers symptom only
lp_signals_detected.rubric_match: L1: 'Compile error calling instance method from main. Cannot explain the static/instance distinction.'

lp_signals_detected.signal: Has no model of static-vs-instance separation
lp_signals_detected.evidence: Treats 'inside the class' as the relevant boundary; no awareness that there are two kinds of context (class-level and object-level)
lp_signals_detected.rubric_match: Below L2: L2 requires 'Knows static methods cannot directly call instance methods. Knows to create an object first.'

expert_benchmark_key_ideas: There are two kinds of method in Java: class-level (static) and object-level (instance). They live in different contexts.
static main runs without any Student object existing. There is no 'self' to call getName() on.
To call getName(), you first need a Student object: 'Student s = new Student(); s.getName();' — then there's an object whose name to get.
Same class does not mean same context. The class is the blueprint; instances are the houses. main exists in the blueprint office; getName() lives in each house.

downstream_effects.stage_2_intervention_filter: L1→L2 interventions: blueprint_vs_house_analogy (matches 'two kinds of method' framing), class_diagram_with_static_marker, instantiation_walkthrough.

stage_3_prompt_sections.LP-1_current_understanding: Student treats class membership as the access boundary; doesn't yet recognize the static/instance split
stage_3_prompt_sections.LP-2_wrong_model_context: SVI-A. LLM should NOT lead with 'static' as a keyword definition — that's the L2 entry point. Lead with 'when does main run? does Sneha exist yet?' to make the gap visible.
stage_3_prompt_sections.LP-3_target_understanding: By end of session: Sneha can produce a fix using 'new Student().getName()' or move getName() to static, and explain in her own words why one or the other is needed.
